source_paper_title: Sparse Integer Programming Is Fixed-Parameter Tractable
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Eisenbrand_2025, title={Sparse Integer Programming Is Fixed-Parameter Tractable}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2023.0162}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2023.0162}, number={3}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Eisenbrand, Friedrich and Hunkenschröder, Christoph and Klein, Kim-Manuel and Koutecký, Martin and Levin, Asaf and Onn, Shmuel}, year={2025}, month=aug, pages={2141–2156} }
context:
Section 7 (Concluding remarks), page 27

In Section 5, we have given a structural characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_1(A)$ bounded by showing that $g_1(A)$ is bounded if and only if $A$ is row-equivalent to a matrix with small dual tree-depth and small entry complexity. Unfortunately, a similar (if and only if) characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_{\infty}(A)$ bounded does not seem to be in our reach.
Problem 1. Find a structural characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_{\infty}(A)$ bounded.
title: Characterize matrices with bounded Graver basis infinity norm under row operations
background_latex:
Let $A\in \mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ be a rational matrix. The integer kernel of $A$ is
\[\ker_{\mathbb{Z}}(A)=\{x\in \mathbb{Z}^n: Ax=0\}.\]
For $x,y\in \mathbb{Z}^n$, write $x\sqsubseteq y$ if (i) $x$ and $y$ lie in the same orthant, i.e., $x_i y_i\ge 0$ for all $i\in [n]$, and (ii) $|x_i|\le |y_i|$ for all $i\in [n]$. The Graver basis $\mathcal{G}(A)$ is the set of $\sqsubseteq$-minimal nonzero vectors in $\ker_{\mathbb{Z}}(A)$.
Define
\[ g_{\infty}(A)=\max\{\|x\|_{\infty}: x\in \mathcal{G}(A)\},\qquad \|x\|_{\infty}=\max_{i\in [n]} |x_i|.\]
Two matrices $A,A'\in \mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ are row-equivalent if one can be obtained from the other by elementary row operations (multiplying a row by a nonzero scalar, or adding a scalar multiple of one row to another). Row-equivalent matrices have the same integer kernel, hence the same Graver basis and the same value of $g_{\infty}(\cdot)$.
A \emph{structural characterization} here means a description, in terms of identifiable structural properties/invariants of $A$ (e.g., graph- or matroid-theoretic parameters associated with $A$, or existence of a row-equivalent matrix with certain sparsity/depth/encoding properties), that is equivalent to the boundedness of $g_{\infty}(A)$ over the class under consideration.
problem_definition_latex:
Find conditions on a rational matrix $A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ that are equivalent to the boundedness of $g_{\infty}(A)$, i.e., characterize the class of matrices for which there exists a function $F$ (depending only on the characterized structural parameters) such that
\[ g_{\infty}(A) \le F. \]
Equivalently, provide an if-and-only-if structural description of those $A$ for which $g_{\infty}(A)$ is bounded (as a finite number) by a bound determined solely by the proposed structural invariants.

Let $A\in \mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ be a rational matrix. The integer kernel of $A$ is
\[\ker_{\mathbb{Z}}(A)=\{x\in \mathbb{Z}^n: Ax=0\}.\]
For $x,y\in \mathbb{Z}^n$, write $x\sqsubseteq y$ if (i) $x$ and $y$ lie in the same orthant, i.e., $x_i y_i\ge 0$ for all $i\in [n]$, and (ii) $|x_i|\le |y_i|$ for all $i\in [n]$. The Graver basis $\mathcal{G}(A)$ is the set of $\sqsubseteq$-minimal nonzero vectors in $\ker_{\mathbb{Z}}(A)$.
Define
\[ g_{\infty}(A)=\max\{\|x\|_{\infty}: x\in \mathcal{G}(A)\},\qquad \|x\|_{\infty}=\max_{i\in [n]} |x_i|.\]
Two matrices $A,A'\in \mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ are row-equivalent if one can be obtained from the other by elementary row operations (multiplying a row by a nonzero scalar, or adding a scalar multiple of one row to another). Row-equivalent matrices have the same integer kernel, hence the same Graver basis and the same value of $g_{\infty}(\cdot)$.
A \emph{structural characterization} here means a description, in terms of identifiable structural properties/invariants of $A$ (e.g., graph- or matroid-theoretic parameters associated with $A$, or existence of a row-equivalent matrix with certain sparsity/depth/encoding properties), that is equivalent to the boundedness of $g_{\infty}(A)$ over the class under consideration.

Find conditions on a rational matrix $A\in\mathbb{Q}^{m\times n}$ that are equivalent to the boundedness of $g_{\infty}(A)$, i.e., characterize the class of matrices for which there exists a function $F$ (depending only on the characterized structural parameters) such that
\[ g_{\infty}(A) \le F. \]
Equivalently, provide an if-and-only-if structural description of those $A$ for which $g_{\infty}(A)$ is bounded (as a finite number) by a bound determined solely by the proposed structural invariants.
open_problem_quote:
In Section 5, we have given a structural characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_1(A)$ bounded by showing that $g_1(A)$ is bounded if and only if $A$ is row-equivalent to a matrix with small dual tree-depth and small entry complexity. Unfortunately, a similar (if and only if) characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_{\infty}(A)$ bounded does not seem to be in our reach.
Problem 1. Find a structural characterization of matrices $A$ with $g_{\infty}(A)$ bounded.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally generate families of matrices and compute $g_{\infty}(A)$ for small sizes (via Graver basis computation) to test candidate characterizations, but the search space is large and Graver basis computation scales poorly, limiting brute-force counterexample hunting to small instances.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate structural characterizations can be encoded as computable features (graph/matroid parameters, depth measures, sparsification via row operations), with a fitness signal based on agreement with computed $g_{\infty}(A)$ on sampled instances. However, computing $g_{\infty}(A)$ and relevant invariants may be expensive, and the space of plausible characterizations is broad and hard to parameterize tightly.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
The provided source paper (Briański–Koutecký–Král’–Pekárková–Schröder, 2023) makes substantial progress on row-operation-invariant structural characterizations, but for \(\ell_1\)-type measures rather than \(g_\infty\). It identifies matroid depth parameters of the column matroid \(M(A)\) that exactly capture the minimum primal and dual tree-depth achievable by row operations: \(\mathrm{td}^*_P(A)=\mathrm{dd}(M(A))\) and (building on earlier work) \(\mathrm{td}^*_D(A)=\mathrm{c}^*\mathrm{d}(M(A))\). It then proves that bounded circuit \(\ell_1\)-norm \(c_1(A)\) and bounded Graver \(\ell_1\)-norm \(g_1(A)\) are functionally equivalent, yielding an if-and-only-if characterization of bounded \(g_1(A)\) in terms of existence of a row-equivalent matrix with bounded dual tree-depth and bounded entry complexity.

For the user’s target invariant \(g_\infty(A)\), the same paper explicitly states that an analogous structural characterization is not currently known and formulates it as an open problem. It also rules out naive candidates: even bounded incidence tree-depth does not imply bounded \(g_\infty(A)\), via explicit families where a Graver element like \((t-1,1,\dots,1)\) appears. Existing sufficient conditions (e.g., bounded primal tree-depth plus bounded entry complexity, as in Koutecký–Levin–Onn) remain only one direction, and the matroidal depth parameters that fully explain row-equivalent primal/dual sparsification do not yet translate into necessary-and-sufficient conditions for bounded \(g_\infty\).

Overall, the state of the art suggests that any characterization of bounded \(g_\infty(A)\) must go beyond depth/sparsity of row-equivalent forms and incorporate additional invariants controlling amplification of coefficients in Graver-minimal kernel vectors. Promising directions include relating \(g_\infty\) to circuit \(\ell_\infty\)-complexity \(c_\infty(A)\), imbalance/denominator parameters (e.g., \(\dot\kappa_A\)), or matroid invariants sensitive to coefficient growth rather than just support structure; however, no if-and-only-if theorem comparable to the \(g_1\) theory is currently available.

sources.title: Characterization of matrices with bounded Graver bases and depth parameters and applications to integer programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Gives matroid-theoretic characterizations of when a matrix is row-equivalent to one of bounded primal tree-depth (via deletion-depth) or bounded dual tree-depth (via contraction*-depth), and proves functional equivalence between bounded circuit \(\ell_1\)-norm \(c_1(A)\) and bounded Graver \(\ell_1\)-norm \(g_1(A)\). It explicitly states that an analogous if-and-only-if characterization for bounded \(g_\infty(A)\) is open.
sources.connection: Directly addresses the sought row-operation-invariant structural characterizations, but only for \(g_1\) (and depth parameters). It formulates the open problem of characterizing bounded \(g_\infty\), which is essentially the user’s target (bounded Graver \(\ell_\infty\)-norm) and provides obstacles/counterexamples for naive depth-based characterizations.

sources.title: Matrices of optimal tree-depth and row-invariant parameterized algorithm for integer programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Introduces/establishes row-invariant matroidal characterization of the minimum dual tree-depth among row-equivalent matrices: \(\mathrm{td}^*_D(A)=\mathrm{c}^*\mathrm{d}(M(A))\), enabling row-invariant FPT algorithms for IP based on dual tree-depth.
sources.connection: Provides the key template: row-equivalence invariants expressed via matroid depth parameters. While it does not characterize bounded \(g_\infty\), it is foundational for any attempt to characterize bounded Graver norms under row operations.

sources.title: Matrices of optimal tree-depth and a row-invariant parameterized algorithm for integer programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Journal version strengthening the ICALP'20 results on row-invariant dual tree-depth via contraction*-depth and algorithmic consequences for IP.
sources.connection: Same as the ICALP version; supplies detailed proofs and is the main reference for the matroidal invariant \(\mathrm{c}^*\mathrm{d}\) controlling optimal dual tree-depth under row operations.

sources.title: A parameterized strongly polynomial algorithm for block structured integer programs
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows that bounded primal tree-depth \(\mathrm{td}_P(A)\) and bounded entry size imply a bound on \(g_\infty(A)\), and bounded dual tree-depth implies a bound on \(g_1(A)\); provides FPT/strongly polynomial algorithms for IP in these regimes.
sources.connection: Gives sufficient conditions for bounded \(g_\infty\) (via primal tree-depth) but not necessary ones; serves as a baseline for what a full characterization of bounded \(g_\infty\) should generalize beyond depth parameters.

sources.title: Integer programming and incidence treedepth
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies IP parameterized by incidence treedepth; includes hardness phenomena and examples where small incidence treedepth does not control \(g_\infty\) without further restrictions.
sources.connection: Provides counterexample patterns and motivates that characterizing bounded \(g_\infty\) cannot rely solely on incidence-depth-like sparsity measures, aligning with the open problem’s difficulty.
